Quickness  

Description

1: Increase Attack Speed by 20% (L15) to 50% (L21)

Details   Raw Spell Data, Lucy Spell View

Mana: 80 Skill: Alteration
Casting Time: 3.1 Recast Time: 1.5
Fizzle Time: 1.5 Range: 100
Location: Any Time of Day: Any
Fizzle Adj: 25 Deletable: Yes
Blockable: Yes Focusable: Yes
Dispellable: Yes Interruptable: Yes
Short Buff Box: No Target Type: Single
Spell Type: Beneficial Source: Live 12/03

Messages

Cast on you: You feel much faster.
Cast on other: Soandso feels much faster.
Effect Fades: Your speed returns to normal.

Game Description

Grants your target quickened movement, increasing their attack speed for 4.0 mins @L15 to 11.0 mins @L50.

Items with this effect

  • Potion of Victory
  • Sharkhide Shawl
  • Spell: Quickness

Quick Facts

Scroll:
Spell: Quickness
ClassLevel
SHM26
ENC15
Expansion:

Duration:
4.0 mins @L15 to 11.0 mins @L50
  • Comments (11)
  • Sold By (16)
  • Quested (1)
  • NPCs with Ability (11)

Post Comment
Haste max percentage out of date
QuoteReply
# Apr 04 2019 at 4:19 PM Rating: Decent
Maitixmikado
4 posts
Not sure how long Quickness has been affected for but the percentage does not come close to 50% by level 21 anymore. As soon as I got the spell on my level 26 shaman alt, I fired this up on my tank merc and it was 30%. I assumed the max level for receiving 50% haste was bumped so I jumped on my level 76 enchanter to verify and it is still 30%. Please update.
▲ Page top
Haste max percentage out of date
QuoteReply
# Apr 04 2019 at 8:50 PM Rating: Excellent
Gidono
View User Forum
Moderator
6,835 posts
Maitixmikado wrote:
Not sure how long Quickness has been affected for but the percentage does not come close to 50% by level 21 anymore. As soon as I got the spell on my level 26 shaman alt, I fired this up on my tank merc and it was 30%. I assumed the max level for receiving 50% haste was bumped so I jumped on my level 76 enchanter to verify and it is still 30%. Please update.


All of the haste spells are parsed out using a ranged formula on our spell parser. The end result is incorrect and is a known bug that I intend to fix. Thanks for the report though.
____________________________
Allakhazam Developer and Admin

Works nice with languid pace
QuoteReply
# Oct 02 2004 at 11:32 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592253217760
View User Forum
65 posts
Enchanters getting this should use this on their pets (or group members) while using languid pace on their opponents. Very good combo. Combined with the weaken and enfeeblement series this will totally wreak havoc on most enemies.

▲ Page top
RE: Quick question
QuoteReply
# May 23 2005 at 5:25 AM Rating: Decent
animekenji
1,123 posts
Yes. Spell haste will stack with worn haste.

If you were me and I were you, would we still be friends?
▲ Page top
Haste trick
QuoteReply
# Oct 20 2003 at 9:30 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592259661450
47 posts
Since times are changing i better update this..

This is a tip is to all you Shamans out there and your the the main haster in a group and have problems timing the recaste time on the haste.

1. Make sure You get a level 34 spell called "Companion Spirit" before you attempt this.

2. Make sure you practiced Conj well enough and can able to summon your pet with out much mana loss. If you keep getting fizzles then try summoning breads and flasks while lfg or in a med break since it won't take much mana.

3. Summon your pet, then cast haste buff onto your pet first or at least the first 4 buff windows on your pet bar, then recast haste on the melees and/or other pet(s)in your group.

What happens now is that it will give you a timer in your pet bar, Just move mouse cursor on it to see the remaining time and when it dies it give you an message "Your pet's Quickness has worn off" or something simular or the picture on pet bar will fade.

When the haste buff dies you will be first to know about it and this will save you alot of time and hassle to ask Melees to tell you if the haste has died.

Now you just repeat the buffs cycle which is, pet first, Melees second, (and if you want to)group member's pets last.

This is good since your not wasting mana since you're helping your pet attack speed and the also melees can focus on the battle instead of checking the spell bar.

Enchanters can also do this earlier then shamans at level 16.

Edited, Sun Oct 17 12:37:15 2004
▲ Page top
RE: Haste trick
QuoteReply
# Oct 31 2005 at 2:11 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591801176674
View User Forum
Scholar
67 posts
Cast all buffs on yourself before anyone. your group will love you since you can start refreshing (or at least announce that you know buffs are dropping and will refresh as mana permits) as soon as you see yours begin to blink. It takes a little extra mana, but most buffs are long term and the extra haste isn't enough mana to worry about.

This will allow you to monitor all buffs and not have to watch for the wear off message through the battle spam.

Note: if low on mana when refreshing haste mid-battle, do not cast on self. cast on tanks, then med up and refresh all around starting with yourself to reset your timer.
